channel.title: summarizedStoriesMainNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesMainNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: 2 Minute Medicine Rewind June 8, 2026
item.description: Food coloring additives and their link to type 2 diabetes incidence in the NutriNet-Santé cohort study.

item.title: Defective HIV copies explain most persistent traces in blood following treatment, study finds
item.description: Antiretroviral therapy allows most HIV patients to live long lives, but defective viral copies remain in the blood.

item.title: Why Cosmetic Surgery Abroad Can Backfire
item.description: Lower prices attract patients abroad for cosmetic surgery, but risks include variable expertise and limited follow-up care.

item.title: Hospitalists Leading Health Informatics in the Digital Age
item.description: Rapidly evolving hospital technology benefits from clinician leadership, according to Medscape Medical News.
